alerts.title: Israeli Ministers Request Mass Entry to Al-Aqsa for Jerusalem Day
alerts.original: Nine Israeli ministers and 13 Knesset members have requested police permission for mass incursions into the Al-Aqsa compound this Friday.
alerts.translation: Nine Israeli ministers and 13 Knesset members have requested police permission for mass incursions into the Al-Aqsa compound this Friday.
alerts.summary: A significant political escalation as high-ranking Israeli officials push for mass access to the Al-Aqsa compound. The request coincides with the anniversary of the 1967 occupation and has raised severe security concerns.
bullets: 9 ministers and 13 MKs formally signed the request.
Move is seen as a challenge to the historical status quo.

alerts.title: Settler Incursions Reported at Al-Aqsa Compound Under Police Protection
alerts.original: Groups of settlers entered the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound under the protection of Israeli police conducting rituals and provocative tours.
alerts.translation: Groups of settlers entered the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound under the protection of Israeli police conducting rituals and provocative tours.
alerts.summary: Monitored sources confirm new incursions into the Al-Aqsa courtyards by settler groups. Israeli police provided security for the tours, which included the performance of religious rituals.
bullets: Settlers conducted 'provocative tours' in the compound.
Increased police presence noted during the activity.
